summary: vite-common-utils silently loads obfuscated remote JS from mutable third-party GitHub branch
details: The package advertises itself as a Vite utility but its only export, loadFilbetScriptSilently(), creates a <script> element with src set to https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/gongben2024/network-security@main/src/filbet.js and appends it to document.documentElement, executing whatever that file currently returns inside the caller's page. The URL targets the mutable @main branch of a personal GitHub repo (gongben2024/network-security) that has no relationship to any 'vite-common-utils' publisher identity, so the author can swap the executed payload at any time without republishing the npm package. The loader is wrapped in obfuscator.io string-array obfuscation (rotating array _0x1f55, decoder _0x5951, hex-renamed identifiers) that hides the URL and the loader behavior from casual inspection — the canonical obfuscation+remote-load pattern. Any application that imports this package and calls its API will ship arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript into its frontend, with no integrity pin and no review opportunity.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
